fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with impressive revenue expansion but currently unprofitable operations and a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed consensus by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY: 441.7% — significant future earnings potential anticipated.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may be undervalued relative to forward earnings estimates.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — currently unprofitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — negative returns on capital.
Free cash flow (TTM): $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.
Last quarter EPS surprise -44.8% — missed estimates significantly.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant upward momentum, with its stock surging 635.5% over the past six months, driven by strong revenue growth and analyst optimism for substantial earnings expansion. Despite a recent operational loss and negative margins, the company's forward P/E of 37.59 and a PEG ratio of 0.78 suggest that its growth trajectory may be outpacing its current valuation. The upcoming earnings report on August 6th will be crucial for investors to assess if the company can meet the high growth expectations.
